name: Veterinary Records & DEA Controlled Substances (US)
description: Reference policy aligned with state veterinary board record-keeping rules and the DEA controlled-substances framework. Tune retention to the state of practice (3–7 years typical).

data.text: Records & controlled substances — state board / DEA aligned
Records must be detailed enough for another licensed veterinarian to continue care.
Patient records are retained per the state of practice — typically 3 to 7 years from the date of the last visit.
Informed consent is documented for invasive procedures, anaesthesia, euthanasia, and significant off-label drug use.
Controlled substances are managed in line with DEA requirements: Schedule II receipts kept ≥2 years, biennial inventory, and theft/loss reported on DEA Form 106.
A valid veterinarian–client–patient relationship (VCPR) is established and documented before prescribing.
Each entry identifies the signing veterinarian; corrections by addendum only.

clauses.title: Records sufficient for handover
clauses.body: You must keep records detailed enough for another licensed veterinarian to continue care.

clauses.title: State retention met
clauses.body: You must retain records for the period required by the state veterinary board (commonly 3–7 years).

clauses.title: Informed consent documented
clauses.body: Informed consent must be documented for invasive procedures, anaesthesia, euthanasia, and significant off-label use.

clauses.title: DEA controlled-substances rules
clauses.body: Controlled substances must be tracked per DEA: Schedule II receipts retained ≥2 years, biennial inventory, theft/loss reported on Form 106.

clauses.title: Valid VCPR before prescribing
clauses.body: You must establish and document a valid VCPR before prescribing.

clauses.title: Veterinarian sign-off identified
clauses.body: Each entry identifies the signing veterinarian; corrections by addendum only.
